text: Hello, Catherine here from BBC Learning English.
Just so you know, this programme is from the BBC Learning English Archive.
It was originally broadcast in June 2008 on our website. Enjoy!
bbclearningenglish .com
about something called ghostwriting.
And with me to look at some of the language to do with this topical topic is Callum Robertson. Hi Callum.
Hi Amber. Callum, the verb to ghostwrite has nothing whatsoever to do with ghost stories, ghost
towns or even ghost trains, does it? No, nothing at all.
When we're talking about ghostwriting and to ghostwrite,
it means to write a book or an article for another person.
So it seems that that person wrote that book, but actually somebody else wrote it for them. And these days it's quite common.
I think many people might expect that books by celebrities particularly have actually been written by somebody else.
They've been written by a ghostwriter. Now, today's question.
The word autobiography comes from the Greek for
A, life pictures, B, life and
self, or C, self, life and right.
Callum, what do you think? Well, I don't know ancient Greek or even modern Greek for that matter, but I would have a guess.
I think I can probably work this out because of auto, I think that's to do with the self.
Bio is life and graphy, you know, it's graphology is writing.
So I would say self, life and write. I think that's what autobiography means.
Thank you. Well, we'll find out whether you're right at the end of the programme.
Now, the singer George Michael has recently signed a multi -million pound deal
for a no holds barred autobiography which he will write entirely himself
in other words he's not going to use a ghost writer
no holds barred Callum what does that three word adjective mean
well it's a good expression and here it means that nothing will be hidden
if it's no holds barred everything will be revealed
now George Michael has had quite a colorful colourful life and no holds barred
autobiography will be very interesting for many people to get his side of some of these colourful stories from his past.
Thank you. Well whatever kind of book George Michael writes
it's likely he will get some help from his agent and his publisher because that's how the publishing process works.
So why do celebrities use ghost writers to write their life stories.
Listen now to Joel Ricketts, an expert on the UK book industry.
Why does he think a ghostwriter may make a better job of a celebrity autobiography than the celebrity themselves?
Ghostwriters in many cases can actually capture the essence of the celebrity's character
and their voice better than the celebrity could themselves if they were actually trying to write it.
What makes these books sell is the sense that you're sitting down
Now, one -on -one, you're having a cup of tea or a beer with this celebrity, and you need to feel like it's really them that you know.
And actually, if they're kind of trying to write it in their own stilted style, that doesn't work as well,
whereas if you've got a professional there, they can actually capture that voice, that tone and that comedy,
and, as I say, craft a book in a much more coherent way.
So, Joel says there that ghostwriters often make a better job
of a celebrity autobiography than the celebrity themselves.
And he says that's because celebrities aren't professional writers
and so they need help to be able to put their voice over on paper.
And a professional writer can do that better than a professional celebrity.
That's right. And the marketing of these best -selling celebrity autobiographies, or memoirs as they're sometimes called,
is often about how intimate these books are, how they'll take you very close to the celebrity and reveal private and personal things about
them. You're listening to bbclearningenglish
.com. Let's hear again from Joel, and this time he talks about readers' attitudes to ghosted celebrity memoirs.
By the way, he says if you read the small print, you'll see the ghostwriter, or ghost, credited.
That means their name appears somewhere on the book to acknowledge their contribution.
As you listen, try to catch whether Joel thinks ghostwriting is becoming more acceptable to readers.
If they looked closely, they would see that the ghost is credited. I mean, it isn't...
I think ghostwriting itself is becoming more mainstream.
The ghosts are sort of stepping out of the closet. In fact, Robert Harris has written a whole novel about a ghost.
And I think readers are getting a bit savvier. I think they don't mind.
as long as they understand that it's the celebrity's life, the celebrity's story, and it's still coming from the celebrity.
So Joel says ghostwriting is becoming more mainstream. It's becoming less shocking because more people know about it.
And he says the ghosts are sort of stepping out of the closet. Can you explain that, Callum?
Well, a closet is a wardrobe, somewhere you keep your clothes, and if something is in the closet, it means it's hidden.
People don't know about it. But if something steps out of the closet, it becomes well known.
It becomes not hidden anymore. So the ghosts are stepping out of the closet.
So people are now aware. They know that there are ghost writers and they accept that there are ghost writers.
Now it's time to say, Callum, you got the right answer to our question at the beginning of the programme.
The word autobiography comes from the Greek for self, life and right.
Do join us next time for more 6 Minute English. Goodbye.
